title: The AI Breakup Mediator — When Your Ex Hired an Algorithm
script: By twenty forty-one, breaking up had become genuinely complicated. Couples shared AI companions, joint smart-home ecosystems, merged social media identities, intertwined biometric profiles, and emotionally bonded pet-translation accounts. Separating two digital lives was harder than divorcing two legal ones. So a legal-tech startup called SplitAI launched the first AI Breakup Mediator. An algorithm that analyzed the entire digital relationship. Messages, shared photos, financial entanglements, emotional dependency data from mood implants. And it produced a mathematically optimal separation agreement in forty-eight hours. The AI was unnervingly good. It knew who was more emotionally attached from biometric data. It knew who had been checked out longer from declining message warmth scores. And it knew who was lying about wanting to stay friends, from micro-expression analysis during the mediation video call. Eighty-seven percent of users reported that SplitAI's assessment was, quote, painfully accurate. But then there was the thirteen percent who rejected the findings. A woman in London went viral after screaming at her SplitAI readout. The algorithm said she stopped loving him in February. She insisted it was March. The AI had timestamped the end of love more precisely than the human who had felt it. The final readout divided assets sixty-one to thirty-nine. The sixty-one percent recipient had loved more. Which raises a question nobody had previously thought to ask. Can you put a percentage on devotion. SplitAI thought so. And honestly, the numbers were hard to argue with.

storytelling.cold_open_candidate: The algorithm told her the exact date she stopped loving him.

open_loops.text: how can an algorithm mediate a breakup
what does painfully accurate mean for breakup data
the thirteen percent who rejected the findings

Escalation hero — the relationship warmth graph declining
From data analysis to human emotional fallout

but_therefore_transitions: SplitAI was unnervingly good — 87 percent said painfully accurate BUT the 13 percent who rejected the findings
the algorithm timestamped the end of love more precisely than the human THEREFORE can you put a percentage on devotion

scenes.prompt: Hyper-realistic photograph of a modern apartment living room circa 2041 split down the middle with an invisible line. On the left side, a jaw-droppingly gorgeous woman in her early 30s sits on a slightly worn grey couch surrounded by personal items, throw pillows, a houseplant that needs watering. On the right side, the couch cushion is conspicuously empty with an indent where someone used to sit. A smart home display on the wall shows a notification SPLITAI MEDIATION SESSION SCHEDULED. The apartment has warm but slightly harsh mixed lighting, mismatched furniture from different decades, a cracked smart device screen on the coffee table. Lived-in environment, realistic wear and tear. Wide landscape composition. 8k resolution, cinematic lighting, documentary style realism, highly detailed, shot on 35mm lens, photorealistic. No painting.

A couple in their 30s sit in separate rooms of the same apartment, each facing a glowing screen circa 2041. The woman, jaw-droppingly gorgeous with tired eyes and messy hair, sits on a couch with a laptop showing a calm AI avatar. In the next room visible through an open doorway, a ruggedly handsome man sits at a desk facing his own screen showing the same AI avatar. Both screens display charts and graphs of their relationship data. The apartment is lived-in with personal clutter, mismatched furniture, coffee mugs on surfaces. Warm evening lighting from mixed sources. Neither person speaks. The mood is tense and quiet. A close-up of a holographic chart floating above a slightly worn desk surface, circa 2041. The chart is labeled RELATIONSHIP WARMTH INDEX and shows a declining line graph spanning twelve months. The line starts warm red at the left labeled JANUARY and steadily drops through orange, yellow, to cold blue. A glowing marker appears at a sharp drop point labeled LOVE ENDED FEBRUARY 14 2041. A woman's hand reaches toward the marker, her finger trembling slightly. The desk surface has scratches, a pen with a chewed cap, and a tissue box. Cool blue light from the hologram mixes with warm overhead lighting. Wide landscape composition. 8k resolution, cinematic lighting, documentary style realism, highly detailed, shot on 35mm lens, photorealistic. No painting.

Hyper-realistic photograph of a SplitAI results screen on a tablet, circa 2041. The screen displays EMOTIONAL ATTACHMENT ANALYSIS. Below them text reads DECEPTION PROBABILITY Partner B claims desire to remain friends PROBABILITY OF SINCERITY 12 PERCENT. The tablet rests on a bedside table next to a glass of water with lipstick on the rim, a bottle of sleeping pills, and a framed photo face-down. Subdued bedroom lighting from a single lamp.
